entry.title: SAP Developer News December 18th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Joule Studio Agent Builder Released 

  • Joule Studio Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/Joule_Studio?locale=en-US 
  • Webinars on “SAP TechEd Takeaways: Unlocking Agentic AI with Joule Studio in SAP Build”: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5132226&sessionid=1&key=68EAF8F96311AF0C06B557E4B6FAADAD&groupId=6438260&partnerref=sapcom&sourcepage=register 
  • Maria on “Unlocking SAP BTP” Podcast: https://pod.link/990578446/episode/ODE0OGVkZGMtMWQyNy00ZDVlLTgxMDYtODI0YjYyZWY4NTYz?view=apps&sort=popularity (podcast) and https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7-DWT6qd15I(YouTube) 
  • Customer use case for building agent for procurement process: https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2025/12/ecad099c-317f-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html 
  • Setting Up Joule Studio for All Partner Flows and Entitlement Levels (blog): https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/setting-up-joule-studio-for-all-partner-flows-and-entitlement-levels/ba-p/14282498\ 
  • Setting Up Joule Studio for All Customer Flows and Entitlement Levels (blog): https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/setting-up-joule-studio-for-all-customer-flows-and-entitlement-levels/ba-p/14240537 

ABAP Holiday Calendar 2025 

  • LinkedIn Post https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/abap-holiday-calendar-2025-sonja-li%25C3%25A9nard-wampe/  

GenAI Hub now officially LLM provider for LiteLLM 

  • Documentation: https://sap-contributions.github.io/litellm-agentic-examples/ 
  • Devtoberfest Session on LiteLLM: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/connect-to-generative-ai-hub-with-litellm-and-build-ai-agents-with-crewai/ev-p/14217011 

New release 1.18 of the Terraform Provider for SAP BTP 

  • Release info https://github.com/SAP/terraform-provider-btp/releases  
  • BTP CLI and APIs CodeJam section on extracting the CF API endpoint value https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-btp-cli-api-codejam/blob/main/exercises/05-core-services-api-prep/README.md#extracting-the-api-endpoint-value  

CAP Enterprise Analytics 

  • NPM Package Details: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/cds-analytics 

SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime trial restrictions lifted 

  • Carolin’s blog post, updated https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/update-btp-trial-update-cf-runtime-availability/ba-p/14247240  

Devtoberfest Winners Interview - Kanika Chhabra 

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Joule Studio Agent Builder Released 

2:42 ABAP Holiday Calendar 2025 

2:58 GenAI Hub now officialy LLM provider for LiteLLM 

4:24 New release 1.18 of the Terraform Provider for SAP BTP 

5:18 CAP Enterprise Analytics 

6:02 SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime trial restrictions lifted 

6:44 Devtoberfest Winners Interview - Kanika Chhabra 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for December 18th, 2025.

[Daniel] Tomorrow, Joule Studio is expected to release the much-anticipated Agent Builder, where you can create agents for yourself without coding in order to extend Joule. You provide the instructions on the context, as well as a set of skills and other tools, and the agent will analyze, plan, and execute the appropriate actions all by itself. You can specify the LLM you want to use, how much thinking you want the agent to do, as well as several more settings. Included are several features even the beta participants did not get to see, including Vibe coding, where you can use prompts to build your agent step-by-step. You can connect to an MCP server and bring those skills into your agent. And you can create sub-agents that can be called by your main agent. Maria Kondratyeva, a senior product manager who has been working tirelessly to get this release out the door before Christmas, says that you will incur no additional unit cost through the end of January when you deploy and test your new agents. I will leave a set of links to materials about Joule Studio and the Agent Builder, including documentation, webinars, and Maria's recent appearance on the very popular Unlocking SAP BTP podpass. That's a really nice jingle. All the links are in the description. Now that Joule Studio is complete with the Agent Builder and the Skill Builder, I assume now more than ever you'll want to install Joule Studio and get started. Lucky for you, Product Management, specifically Maria and Charin, have produced two blogs on how to set up Joule Studio, one specifically for partners and one specifically for customers. It will show you the SKUs you need for build and Joule. It'll show you the boosters, the formations, the security setup, the roles, and everything that you need for whatever landscape you're using, and whether or not you've already installed Joule Studio or Joule or Process Automation. Links to both of those blogs are also in the description.

[Shilpa] Christmas is around the corner, and the SAP ABAP team brings you ABAP holiday calendar 2025. Check out the LinkedIn post by Sonia, where she highlights the key achievements in the area of ABAP in this year.

[Nora] I have some really cool news for all the SAP AI developers out there. It's basically an early Christmas gift because my friend from development just reached out to me, they told me that generative AI hub is now an official large language model provider for light LLM. Now what is light LLM? Light LLM is an open source library that basically unifies all the different API calls to the different large language model providers. So if it's compatible now with generative AI hub, that means that you can use all the models that we partner with. You can use generative AI hub and light LLM and then all the cool agent development kits, for example, that are compatible with light LLM, and that is really cool because the list is endless. I have those tier because I can't remember everything. So there's LandGraph, Crew AI, Pydantic AI, Google Agent Development Kit, so ADK, OpenAI ADK, AWS TransAgents, Lama Index, Microsoft Agents, and that's not even the whole list. It goes on and on and on. So make sure you check out the documentation that is linked in the show notes wherever they appear. And then also check out the Devtoberfest session with my colleague Karim, who also linked a GitHub repository, where he shows how you can build your own AI agents with generative AI Hub, LightLLM and Crew AI. And yeah, happy coding over the holidays. Happy holidays and happy coding.

[DJ] The team behind the Terraform provider for SAP BTP has an early holiday season present for us. A new version, 1.18, has just landed, bringing fixes, improvements, and new features. Here are just three of the highlights. So first, resources and data sources for destinations are now available. MTLS authentication for service brokers is now supported and there are also some new functions for extracting information from environment instance labels, if you know you know. Check out the full release details for these and more features. By the way, there's already a patch version 1.18.1 which you should use. Links in the description.

[Tom] This month we see a great new CAP add-in in the form of the SAP enterprise analytics add-in. This cool new little feature brings to CAP the support for INA services or information access. These are particularly useful when you want to consume a service from the SAP Analytics Cloud, particularly if you have a calculation view. It's very easy to use: drop in the record referred to module, NPN module, and then simply add some annotations to your data model. It's that simple to now add INA services. So if you're interested in that functionality, be sure to check it out today.

[DJ] A few weeks back, we had a news item on access being temporarily restricted for SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtimes in the trial environment. Just a few days ago, the SAP BTP trial product team announced that these restrictions have been lifted again and Cloud Foundry runtimes are now fully available again in trial. That's great news and another early holiday season present for us. A link to Carolyn's original blog post, which now has an update with these details, is in the description.

[Kanika Chhabra] 'm so happy to have one of our winners from Devtoberfest. So give me this car. I did happy day possible. Oh yeah, she take away it out of that. High ah, so many like that. I did not have a lot of tech background but I was able to do the exercises and MPK, so it was really intuitive and everything was really well developed. So I didn't need to have a lot of background knowledge because one cannot know everything about everything. So it was really very made. That's what's really was raised. And now you are here in Berlin, One TechEd. So what did you like most or what you're looking for to get out of here? So I just got a little interested in SAP Build and I found out all the SAP Build hands-on workshops here, and that really helped me. So I do not have a prior experience on that but I started from scratch here working on the SAP Build and I was actually able to learn a lot and now I have a certification made on SAP BuilderXS. Yeah, so I have like exciting about that. Very impressive. Thank you so much.

entry.title: Clean Core maturity and the new Extensibility Levels
entry.content:
For years, the architectural conversation around SAP S/4HANA seemed trapped in a rigid side.
On one side, the purist vision moving all development to side-by-side extensibility on SAP BTP. On the other side, the operational reality, where complex transactional logic and proximity to data made decoupling code technically expensive and sometimes unfeasible..

However, we often lost in this conversation: Clean Core... what's side-by-side... what's On-stack
Not so far back in time, we followed the 3-Tier Development Model. This framework tried to organize development into three tiers (Tier 1 for Cloud, Tier 2 with Wrappers, Tier 3 for Legacy).

While useful, there's a new pragmatic second generation framework: the Clean Core Level Concept
This new maturity model increases the availability of APIs and reduces the forced need for wrappers, incorporating classic ABAP use cases safely. But how does this translate into the modern architecture of Levels A, B, C, and D?

Level A: The Gold Standard (On-stack & Side-by-side)
Level A doesn't force you off-stack. It represents extensions that rely exclusively on stable, public interfaces backed by stability. Is agnostic to the deployment environment:

Side-by-side Extensions: Decoupled extensions running on the Business Technology Platform. This ecosystem allows for SAP Build (low-code/pro-code), CAP (Java/Node.js), and ABAP Cloud (Steampunk) to create scalable apps and automations

On-stack Extensions: Created inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud (either as Key User Extensions or Developer Extensions). This validates that building tightly coupled logic within the S/4HANA it's ok, provided that released technologies like RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) and CDS Views are used. So that by using of released APIs and strict syntax checks, you can build tightly coupled logic (essential for transactional integrity) without creating technical debt

Level B: The Pragmatism of "Classic APIs"
Unlike Level A, which relies on stability contracts, Level B uses Classic APIs and frameworks. While not strictly "Cloud" capable, these are upgrade-stable.
You can identify these in the Cloudification Repository. Object frameworks such as SAPscript, and ALE/IDoc integrations fall into this category. This provides a critical "safe harbor," allowing to leverage proven legacy technologies without facing immediate refactoring pressure

Level C: Risk Management and Changelogs
When business reality demands the use of internal SAP objects for legacy scenarios, we enter Level C. Unlike the previous levels, there is an upgrade risk here. To mitigate it, SAP provides the Changelog for SAP Objects, a tool that allows you to identify incompatible changes early and plan proactive actions. Although not the preferred approach, it is a valid option if managed with wrappers to isolate the dependency.

Level D : The red line (Technical Debt)
Any extension that uses non-recommended patterns, such as direct modifications to the standard, direct writing to tables, or Implicit Enhancements, falls into this category. This represents the highest risk of technical debt and must be rigorously blocked using ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) checks. These patterns bypass standard logic and guarantee disruption during upgrades

Extensions objects are classified as unfit for customer use (marked as "noAPI" in the Cloudification Repository) 

By understanding Levels (A, B, C, D) we can make architectural decisions that balance technical purity with operational reality, prioritizing business stability and flexibility.... But Important!!: Beyond the levels, a Clean Core strategy requires governance. The fundamental principle is: Extensions must be avoided when possible. Before, we must to evaluate if standard functionality covers the process. If the extension is inevitable, we must ensure that extensions don't break Upgrades
To decide between an On-stack or Side-by-side implementation, customers should use the SAP Application Extension Methodology. This methodology helps to define a custom extension strategy. It addresses challenges like "clean core" by offering a structured, standard approach. This ensures a common understanding and terminology and it allows to assess technical options and make informed decisions to build an extension framework for your organization.

---
Bookmark this SAP Note: 3578329 - Frameworks, Technologies and Development Patterns in Context of Clean Core Extensibility 
Official list of framework and technology classifications that will tell you what is Level A, B, C, or D

New Cloudification Repository Viewer for Clean Core Governance and Development
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/new-cloudification-repository-viewer-for-clean-core-governance-and/ba-p/14236110

entry.title: How to use a CAP application to manage DMQ messages on SAP Advanced Event Mesh
entry.content:
Context

A message that reaches SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh is moved to a Dead Message Queue (DMQ) when it's 'Time to live' (TTL) has passed or when the number of delivery attempts to the consumer has been exceeded. Once the TTL of messages in the DMQ has passed the message broker deletes affected messages irrevocably. Message deletion might not be acceptable in the related integration scenario.

This blog post describes how to create a CAP application that retrieves DMQ messages from SAP Advanced Event Mesh for quarantine. Retrieving DMQ messages from the message broker before their TTL ends safeguards messages from deletion and keeps the messages alive in quarantine. Using management features like payload inspection and correction, quarantined messages can be reinjected into SAP Advanced Event Mesh to reattempt delivery to the original consumer.

Implement a CAP project

Using SAP Build Code, in a Dev Space of type 'Full-Stack Application Using Productivity Tools', create a multi-target application project using the project from template wizard 'Full Stack Project'. The project will use the node.js flavour of CAP. 

Add CAP messaging plugin

To equip your project for interaction with SAP Advanced Event Mesh do configuration as described in plugin cap-js/advanced-event-mesh. Set 'skipManagement' to 'true' because this CAP application is going to consume messages from and produce messages to existing queues. Management access (to create queues, publish or subscribe to topics) to SAP Advanced Event Mesh is not required for this. Use the 'subaccountId' property to indicate the subaccount in which SAP Advanced Event Mesh is subscribed in case you need to deploy your CAP application to another subaccount.

This is what our 'messaging' configuration in the package.json ended up looking:

"messaging": {
  "kind": "advanced-event-mesh",
  "credentials": {
    "user-provided": {
      "name": "advanced-event-mesh"
    }
  },
  "skipManagement": true,
  "subaccountId": "our subaccount that subscribes to SAP Advanced Event Mesh"
}

On CAP application start, the cap-js/advanced-event-mesh plugin will take care of establishing a session with SAP Advanced Event Mesh and auto-renew the OAuth token for the time the CAP server is up. To get access to the session in your application logic, connect to the 'messaging' service

// get access to messaging service instance
const MessagingService = await cds.connect.to('messaging');

and use property MessagingService.session for interaction with SAP Advanced Event Mesh.

CAP schema definition

Using CDS create a DB schema.cds, that caters for entities SourceQueue and QueueMessage. The SourceQueue is for configuration of the DMQs whose messages should be retrieved for quarantine. The QueueMessage entity is for persisting the retrieved DMQ messages in all relevant aspects for inspection and resubmission to SAP Advanced Event Mesh. For the binary payload use CAP type LargeBinary, for the text payload use CAP type LargeString.

This is what our QueueMessage entity ended up looking:

@assert.unique: {queueMessageId: [queueMessageId]}
entity QueueMessage : cuid, managed {
          queueMessageId : String(36) @mandatory;
          queueId        : String(250);
          sourceQueue    : Association to SourceQueue;
          contentType    : String;
          payloadType    : Association to PayloadType;
          payloadLength  : Integer;
          textPayload    : LargeString;
          ingestedAt     : Timestamp;
          messageStatus  : Association to MessageStatus;
          topicId        : String;
          binaryPayload  : LargeBinary;
          eventEmittedAt : Timestamp;
          eventType      : String;
          eventSource    : String;
          eventId        : String;
          acknowledged   : Boolean;
}

CAP service definition

Using CDS create one service whose purpose is to connect a SourceQueue (sourcequeue-connector.cds) and another for managing quarantined messages (queuemessage-manager.cds). In sourcequeue-connector.cds for entity SourceQueue add bound actions openConnection, closeConnection, checkConnection, cleanup and flush. For 'cleanup' we have implemented logic to remove all queue messages that have successfully been resubmitted to SAP Advanced Event Mesh. With 'flush' we cater for resubmission of all messages that remain to be resent to SAP Advanced Event Mesh for the source queue in question. In queuemessage-manager.cds for entity QueueMessage add bound action produce. 

CAP service implementation

As SAP Advanced Event Mesh is powered by the Solace PubSub+ Platform it comes as no surprise that the cap-js/advanced-event-mesh plugin is an implementation of the Solace Node.js Messaging API. This is good to know as with this CAP application we are not letting the plugin publish and subscribe to a CAP app specific topic as it normally would but we'll be consuming and producing from queues instead. So we'll be relying on the CAP messaging plugin merely for establishing the session with SAP Advanced Event Mesh.

Add a sourcequeue-connector.js file in the same location as sourcequeue-connector.cds which CAP by convention of matching names will consider as the module holding custom application logic for the service. For action openConnection of entity SourceQueue implement as per the Solace QueueConsumer sample. Instead of establishing a new session with SAP Advanced Event Mesh though just reuse the one established for you by the CAP plugin:

// create the consumer, specifying the name of the queue
let consumer = new QueueConsumer(solace, sourceQueue, MessagingService.session);

To receive messages add a handler function for the solace.MessageConsumerEventName.MESSAGE event and write all relevant attributes to the QueueMessage entity using CQL INSERT. Opening a connection from CAP will create a consumer on the queue on SAP Advanced Event Mesh message broker. All messages in the queue on the SAP Advanced Event Mesh message broker at the time of establishing a connection and all new messages entering the DMQ while connected will be consumed and acknowledged by the CAP application. 

Add a queuemessage-manager.js file in the same location as queuemessage-manager.cds which CAP by convention of matching names will consider as the module holding custom application logic for the service. For action produce of entity QueueMessage implement as per the Solace QueueProducer sample to write the quarantined message to the target queue on SAP Advanced Event Mesh.

Fiori Element UIs

Using the Storyboard feature in Build Code add 2 separate List Report Page UIs, one consuming the sourcequeue-connector service and one consuming the queuemessage-manager service. Using the sourcequeue-connector UI users can connect to a DMQ on SAP Advanced Event Mesh and manage the connection. If a DMQ is left in connected state then all messages from the related DMQ are immediately quarantined to the CAP application. Using the queuemessage-manager UI users can inspect quarantined messages, validate payloads and resubmit them to SAP Advanced Event Mesh. In our implementation of the CAP application we allow for correcting payloads to cater for situations where the source system cannot reproduce messages.

Fiori Elements visual impressions

This is how our tiles in the launchpad ended up looking:

Here is an impression of the SourceQueue object page of the 'Connect Source Queues' app. Note the buttons for execution of custom actions connect, disconnect, check connection, flush and cleanup from the CDS service definition. The footer action 'Refresh' is a custom action implemented as extension to the Fiori Elements Listreport and Object Page template.

Here is an impression of the QueueMessage object page of the 'Manage Queue Messages' app. Note the button for execution of custom action produce from the CDS service definition.

Note the payload facet that will visualize a textual message body formatted as JSON where applicable. Depending on your requirements make fields editable or disallow editing all together depending on user authorization.

Deploy as Multitarget Application to Cloud Foundry

Deploying to Cloud Foundry in BTP requires the formulation of a development descriptor mta.yaml for the CAP project. Leave the creation of the mta.yaml file to the 'cds add mta, workzone-standard' command to get its base structure right. As the cap-js/advanced-event-mesh CDS plugin requires bindings to a user provided service 'advanced-event-mesh' and an aem-validation-service plan instance add them manually as resources to our mta.yaml.

  #User provided service for cap-js/messaging plugin
  - name: advanced-event-mesh
    type: org.cloudfoundry.user-provided-service

  #Service to validate message broker (VMR) exists
  - name: aem-validation-cap-queuemessagemanager
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: aem-validation-service
      service-plan: aem-validation-service-plan

and refer to them in the 'require' section of the CAP server module.

Special considerations

The following topics deserve special mention as they need to be thought of ahead of deploying the CAP application.

BTP subaccount for deployment

We consider the CAP application to quarantine DMQ messages from SAP Advanced Event Mesh as enterprise support, i.e. a central support function that should be implemented once per global account. That is why we have chosen a central BTP subaccount dedicated to Enterprise Application Integration for deployment of the CAP application, i.e. a subaccount that is not dedicated to a line of business.

SAP Advanced Event Mesh (powered by Solace PubSub+) has a concept of 'environments' with which it separates development, test and production artifacts. All environments are managed through the SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh subscription of the one BTP subaccount. To stick to our approach to use BTP subaccounts for separating development, test and production we use the BTP subaccounts dedicated to the respective stage to deploy the CAP application. It is the user provided service 'advanced-event-mesh' in each subaccount that provides the CAP application with details about the message broker to connect to. Our development, test and production BTP subaccounts trust different SAP Identity and Authentication Service (IAS) instances and the OAuth Profile that we created on the message brokers accordingly ensure that messages cannot be sent across stages.

Rendering through SAP Build Workzone

For deployment of our CAP application for DMQ message management we chose the managed app router approach. Due to the fact that our central enterprise launchpad though is running on another BTP subaccount than the one that holds the deployed CAP application we are exposing it as a 'content provider'. This way we can consume it cross-subaccount from the BTP subaccount that runs our central enterprise launchpad.

entry.title: Custom Retry Wrapper for SAP Cloud SDK HTTP Calls
entry.content:
Custom Retry Wrapper for SAP Cloud SDK HTTP Calls

 

A Technical Justification & Deep Dive

Executive Summary

Most SAP Cloud SDK integrations rely on outbound HTTP calls to external systems. While the SDK provides built-in resilience features such as retries and circuit breakers, real-world integration scenarios often require finer control that goes beyond the default middleware.

To address these requirements, a custom utility function — executeHttpRequestWithRetry() — was implemented using executeHttpRequest combined with async-retry.

What This Wrapper Delivers

  • Precise control over retry behaviour

  • Per-attempt logging with full visibility

  • Business-aware retry logic (not just technical retries)

  • Clear separation of retryable vs. non-retryable responses

  • Fully consistent retry logic across all services

This document explains why this approach was required, how it aligns with SAP Cloud SDK resilience principles, and what benefits it provides.

1. Introduction

SAP Cloud SDK supports resilience patterns such as retries, timeouts, and circuit breakers. However:

These features require explicit configuration and do not cover every business scenario by default.

In complex integrations, inconsistent API behaviour and intermittent failures are common. To ensure robustness and predictable behaviour across projects, a custom retry layer was introduced.

2. Implementation Overview

2.1 Dependency

npm install async-retry

2.2 Retry Wrapper Implementation

// executeHttpRequestWithRetry.ts
import { executeHttpRequest } from '@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client';
import retry from 'async-retry';

export async function executeHttpRequestWithRetry(
  destination: any,
  requestConfig: any,
  options?: any
) {
  return retry(
    async (bail, attempt) => {
      try {
        console.log(`Attempt ${attempt} with URL: ${requestConfig?.url}`);
        return await executeHttpRequest(destination, requestConfig, options);
      } catch (error: any) {
        // Bail out if API returns misleading 2xx status with business error
        if (error?.response?.status?.toString().startsWith('2')) {
          bail(error);
        }

        console.log(`Error in attempt ${attempt}: ${error.message}`);
        throw error;
      }
    },
    {
      retries: 3
    }
  );
}

2.3 Service Usage Example

// service.ts
public async methodName(userId: string) {
  const response = await executeHttpRequestWithRetry(
    { destinationName: SF_DESTINATION_NAME },
    { method: HTTP_METHODS.GET, url: this.buildUrl(userId) },
    { fetchCsrfToken: false }
  );

  return response?.data?.d?.results?.[0] ?? null;
}

3. Why a Custom Wrapper Was Needed

(Based on SAP Cloud SDK Resilience Guidelines)

3.1 Full Control Over Retry Behaviour

SAP Cloud SDK retry middleware follows generic retry patterns. In practice, we needed:

  • A custom retry count

  • Visibility into each retry attempt

  • Explicit control over retryable vs. non-retryable errors

With the custom wrapper, logs such as:

Attempt X with URL: ...

provide full traceability, which is not available by default in the SDK.

3.2 Intelligent, Business-Aware Error Handling

Some APIs return 2xx HTTP status codes even when business logic fails (e.g., partial upserts, invalid payloads).

if (error.response.status starts with 2) bail(error);

This ensures:

  • Misleading 2xx business failures → No retry

  • Genuine technical failures (5xx, network issues) → Retry automatically

SAP Cloud SDK resilience middleware does not provide this level of business-aware filtering.

3.3 Consistent Retry Logic Across Microservices

SDK resilience features must be configured per client or via global providers, which can be missed or misconfigured.

The wrapper guarantees:

  • Identical retry behaviour

  • Uniform error handling

  • Standardised logging

  • Consistent retry count

across the entire codebase, regardless of which team or developer implements a module.

3.4 Simplified Developer Experience

Instead of configuring resilience for every outbound request, developers simply call:

executeHttpRequestWithRetry(...)

This approach:

  • Reduces configuration mistakes

  • Improves maintainability

  • Ensures predictable behaviour

  • Hides resilience complexity from application developers

3.5 Improved Observability

The wrapper explicitly logs:

  • Each retry attempt

  • Target URL

  • Error messages per attempt

SDK default logging often abstracts retry internals unless advanced logging is enabled.

3.6 Future-Proof Design

Using async-retry enables easy future enhancements:

  • Exponential backoff

  • Custom retry delays

  • Conditional retries (e.g., HTTP 429 throttling)

  • Telemetry and monitoring hooks

These enhancements are significantly easier to implement with a custom retry abstraction.

4. Comparison: Custom Wrapper vs. SAP Cloud SDK Defaults

Aspect SAP Cloud SDK (Generic) Custom Wrapper (async-retry)

Retry Status HandlingGeneric, limited customizationExplicit control over 5xx, 429, network errors; fail-fast on 4xx
ObservabilityRetry logic mostly hiddenPer-attempt logging with full transparency
IdempotencyLimited method-level controlEnforced idempotency, with override options
Backoff & JitterFixed defaultsFully configurable (min/max timeout, factor, randomization)

5. Retry Flow (Conceptual)

Request → Attempt 1 → Success → Return

⤷ Failure → Retry Decision → Attempt 2 → ... → Max Retries → Fail or Bail

6. Benefits Summary

Benefit Description

Per-attempt loggingComplete visibility into retry behaviour
Business-aware retriesPrevents retries on misleading 2xx responses
Consistent behaviourUniform logic across services
Simple developer usageOne function call, no configuration
FlexibilityEasy extension for throttling and backoff
Easier debuggingClear insights into API instability

7. Conclusion

While SAP Cloud SDK provides a strong resilience foundation, real-world enterprise integrations demand deeper control and transparency.

The custom executeHttpRequestWithRetry wrapper successfully extends SDK capabilities by delivering:

  • Predictable and consistent retry logic

  • Business-aware error handling

  • High observability

  • Simplified developer experience

  • Flexibility for future enhancements

This approach ensures reliable, maintainable, and production-grade integrations across all SAP Cloud SDK-based services.

entry.title: SAP Developer News January 8th,, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

CAP December 2025 release 

  • CAP December 2025 release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/dec25  
  • SAP Developer News episode covering Status-Transition Flows and the CAP November 2025 release https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BkCPoH7VKHg  
  • A simple exploration of status transition flows in CAP (blog post) https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2025/12/08/a-simple-exploration-of-status-transition-flows-in-cap/  
  • Axiom AXI001 What not how https://github.com/qmacro/capref/blob/main/axioms/AXI001.md  
  • A new Hands-on SAP Dev mini-series on the core expression language in CDS (blog post) https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2025/12/09/a-new-hands-on-sap-dev-mini-series-on-the-core-expression-language-in-cds/  
  • CDS expressions in CAP - Under the hood - Part 1 (upcoming live stream) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aiE20i5BP70  
  • Best Practice BES006 Compose with reuse in mind https://github.com/qmacro/capref/blob/main/bestpractices/BES006.md  
  • The OData "Common" Vocabulary's FieldControlType definitions https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.html#FieldControlType  

2025 set the pace, 2026 wins the race: ABAP 

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/2025-set-the-pace-2026-wins-the-race-abap-ai-with-joule-vs-code-and-ccm/ba-p/14302433 

What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – 2025-Q4 

  • Overview: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-december-2025/ba-p/14295366 
  • New Machine Learning, NLP and AI features in SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q4: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/new-machine-learning-nlp-and-ai-features-in-sap-hana-cloud-2025-q4/ba-p/14293152 
  • SAP CodeJam on Foundations of AI applications with the SAP HANA Cloud -- ML, Vectors, Knowledge Graph: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407#codejam1  

 Application Vulnerability Report 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-application-vulnerability-report-for-cloudfoundry-applications/ba-p/14281684 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 CAP December 2025 release 

6:20 2025 set the pace, 2026 wins the race: ABAP 

7:22 What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – 2025-Q4 

9:36 Application Vulnerability Report 

10:32 Devtoberfest Winner Jose Pereira 

Transcript

00;00;00;08 - 00;00;05;26

This is the SAP Developer News for January 8th, 2026.

00;00;05;29 - 00;00;09;26

Over the holiday period, the CAP team delivered a nice present for us.

00;00;10;00 - 00;00;44;20

The December 2025 release. Let's take a quick look through the highlights. But before we do, let's check out the new FEATURE STATUS BADGES that are appearing more and more - in fact, there are a few in the notes for this release.

As we all know our Greek alphabet, we can correctly surmise that while BETA features are post-experimental and still possibly subject to change ... GAMMA features, not to be confused with the type of radiation that created The Incredible Hulk, are pretty much finalised and ready to use & test thoroughly.

00;00;44;22 - 00;01;19;15

Two such features that are at GAMMA level in this release are Status-Transition Flows and Declarative Constraints.

We covered Status-Transition Flows in a previous news item for the previous release, and there's an overview in this blog post we have for you.

So let's instead take a brief look at the super exciting Declarative Constraints. There are new possibilities with the @assert annotation to define complex constraints, in your CDS model.

00;01;19;18 - 00;01;50;01

That's right, declaratively. We should always strive to PUSH outwards, pushing logic up into the declarative model, and queries & data manipulation down to the database, which goes hand in hand with CAP’s axiom WHAT NOT HOW.

The conditions are expressed in another domain specific language in CDS - CXL - CDS Expression Language.

00;01;50;01 - 00;02;19;07

And, almost as if we'd planned it, there’s a new Hands-on SAP Dev live stream series on CXL starting Friday next week, with me and one of the fab members of the CAP compiler team, Patrice. Don't miss it!

CAP’s support for hierarchical tree views has just become a lot more streamlined, with a new @hierarchy annotation replacing a ton of OData specific ones.

00;02;19;08 - 00;02;46;26

Now that's what I call simple reuse, and cap is an incredibly important idea and a best practice to add in that context. There's a new command CDS export, with which you can create API client packages from your CDS service. Definitions. The output is a full featured cap reuse package that can be published to and pulled from npm registries.

00;02;46;29 - 00;03;15;23

Now that’s what I call simple.

Reuse in CAP is an incredibly important idea, and best practice too, and in that context, there’s a new command cds export with which you can create API client packages from your CDS service definitions; the output is a full-featured CAP reuse package that can be published to and pulled from NPM registries. If exporting via EDMX is the lossy MP3 audio format, then cds export gives us a lossless transfer, more like FLAC.

By the way, if you're interested to learn more about reuse in CAP, with NPM packages, we've a new blog post series Modules, modularity & reuse in CDS models which you might be interested in, with parts 1 and 2 already published.

00;03;15;26 - 00;03;45;29

In the CDS Language & Compiler topic, there's an enhancement that will appeal to those of you who develop and serve multiple frontends from the app/ directory. Now CDS file content from all ./app/* subdirectories will be fetched and loaded (say, with cds build or cds serve) instead of just from the app/ directory itself. This means we don't have to use an index.cds file in the app/ directory to import what we need.

00;03;46;01 - 00;04;19;26

Staying in the CDS Language & Compiler area, there's another enhancement that allows us to use symbols not only for enums defined in CDS, but now also for enums in OData annotations. The example in the release notes in Capire is great, because not only does it illustrate the enhancement, but it also causes the curious amongst us to use our brains and go to find out where the relation between 1 and Readonly, and between 7 and Mandatory, is defined.

00;04;19;26 - 00;04;51;01

And of course it's in OData's “Common” vocabulary, contributed by SAP.

In the Node.js runtime topic, it is now possible to make direct CRUD requests on draft-enabled entities; for example, instead of the EDIT-PATCH-SAVE sequence, you can now just send a direct UPDATE request.

Also, event queue processing has been consolidated into a default queue ... and custom queues, and there has been a change to the db-related guarantee, from ‘at least once’ to ‘exactly once’.

00;04;51;01 - 00;05;23;03

Related guarantee from at least once to exactly once. Finally, in the NodeJS runtime topic, there has been some cleanup of the model reflection APIs. Some function uses have been deprecated and the text entities are now available in a text property of the corresponding localized entity. Moving to the Java runtime topic, there are some important changes related to deep updates for draft enabled entities.

00;05;23;06 - 00;05;52;09

Finally in the Node.js runtime topic, there has been some clean up of the model reflection APIs. Some function usages have been deprecated, and the .texts entities are now available in a texts property of the corresponding localised entity.

In the Java runtime, there are some important changes related to deep updates for draft-enabled entities.

00;05;52;11 - 00;06;23;25

Now, as well as support for active entities, there’s also deep update support for inactive entities too, as long as the keys are included.

Additionally, there’s a new as(String alias) method on the CqnValue interface; with that, it’s possible to create aliased values directly, making it easier to build queries with aliased values.

In the Tools topic, there’s a Kyma convenience now for the cds up command; if your project contains Helm deployment resources, and no mta.yaml file, then you don't need to specify the --to k8s option explicitly any more.

And last but not least, in the Plugins topic, the Node.js plugin @Pa_Vi/cds-rfc now supports multitenancy.

There are links to everything I've mentioned, and more ... in the description.

00;06;23;27 - 00;06;45;04

In this blog post, Simona reflects on how 2025 laid the groundwork for AI powered ABAP development and gives us a clear line of sight into where things are going in 2026. One big highlight from the blogpost is that Joule for developers will remain free for another year, giving us more time to explore AI assisted coding in real projects.

00;06;45;07 - 00;07;15;00

The blog post walks us through the evolution of AI last year and what's to come. Smart tooling with the AI workflow, deeper integration with VS code, and a renowned focus on custom code migration using AI, all designed to help developers be more productive and modernize their code bases. And that's really the strategy going into 2020. Shipping early, scaling smart, and keeping the ABAP core clean in 2025 is about unlocking what I can do.

00;07;15;07 - 00;07;22;12

Then 2026 will make ABAP AI the core of AI about development.

00;07;22;15 - 00;07;54;05

The Q4 2025 release of SAP HANA Cloud brings many updates designed to streamline your workflow and unlock new possibilities. Let’s dive into what’s new.

Let's start with SAP HANA Cloud Central, which now supports background processing for long-running SQL statements that continue even after closing your browser. A new consolidated view of HDI schemas and containers provides quick access to HDI artifacts and their metadata.

00;07;54;08 - 00;08;36;26

SAP Business Data Cloud integration is streamlined—data products are automatically exposed as virtual tables, reducing manual setup and accelerating data reuse.

Calculation view modeling adds column lineage via SQL table functions, dynamic input parameters for MDS cube conversions, and runtime-evaluated restricted columns. Joins are enhanced with support for left outer joins in star joins.

This release introduces the ability to transform property graphs into semantic knowledge graphs without requiring redesign of data models, enabling flexible switching between paradigms on the same data for your use cases.

00;08;36;28 - 00;09;10;09

For developers and data scientists, AI and machine learning capabilities have undergone significant enhancements. The new Unified Time Series procedure provides a consistent interface across algorithms, simplifying development. Text embedding models have been improved to reduce output dimensions without sacrificing accuracy, and the new cross and convert model. In an NLP service ensures more precise search results. Ranking with the Hana Experiment Monitor user interface.

00;09;10;11 - 00;09;34;05

The final SAP CodeJam of 2025 took place in Istanbul, with a focus on AI in SAP HANA Cloud. The next one will happen in February in Berlin, and rest assured, it will be updated with some of these cool new enhancements. You can request this CodeJam topic in your location also!

00;09;34;08 - 00;09;38;22

Also see all the.

00;09;38;25 - 00;10;08;14

Why not start the year off right? With the cleanest, safest applications you can. That's what the new application vulnerability report service for on SAP. BTP focuses on Cloud Foundry applications. Is exactly for this new service. Now in beta will allow you to scan your running applications on SAP, BTP on a regular basis and look for critical vulnerabilities. Don't wait until something happens to one of your applications to find out that there's a vulnerability in it.

00;10;08;16 - 00;10;31;19

Be proactive and find out in advance. So I encourage you to check out this brand new service. I'll put the link to the blog post that describes it, as well as goes through the setup process. It's very easy to use and you can start getting your reports today, so I would encourage you to do so.

00;10;31;22 - 00;11;00;19

So my bedroom. So I hear that there were the two of us and have got a special leftovers right, to work with the community that long. You know what you want, right? Nope. There was kind of a learning journey that you did. Yeah. Our bosses always incentive us to look forward. And WhatsApp has to offer. Even if we don't do work on it.

00;11;00;21 - 00;11;22;13

It's not right. But if you know it, and that exists once the opportunity arrives, you already know it, then you can hit the ground running. Yeah. That's fantastic. So basically, as part of this learning journey, studying your cell phone, it's a party that you took part in. Definitely. Yeah. So what was your favorite aspect of that for week?

00;11;22;15 - 00;11;52;13

Yeah. I think, seeing the videos, making the quizzes, I was amazed with Joe. Yeah. Cool. Yeah. That's really cool. So did you manage to attend any of the videos live, or are you watching? Sort of, recording? Nope. I had done both. That's dedication. That's real dedication. The journey was only, to learn. We have, a few months just learning and doing things.

00;11;52;15 - 00;12;18;14

Yeah. Yeah. But in terms of, you know, what? What motivates you to keep doing. What is it? What's the secret? I think I'm a person that really wants to know more every time and keep searching and work for we can do. And, how can we automate our work? The aims, I think the I think it was all about that shit.

00;12;18;15 - 00;12;31;06

Oh, yeah. Yeah. How can we make our develop life better? Easier? And also curiosity driving. We got to learn. That's why I think that's the best definition of what developed. Yeah. Pedro, thanks very much. Thank you.

entry.content:
SAP Developer News - YouTube

SAP Open Source Report 2025

Become a CAP Developer series

UI5 MCP Server's New TypeScript Migration Feature - Real-World Experience and Best Practices

CHAPTER TITLES

Transcription

00;00;00;05 - 00;00;06;07

This is the SAP Developer News for January 15th, 2026.

00;00;06;10 - 00;00;09;20

Good morning from the SAP offices in central London well in the

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;34;19

City of London, in fact. The SAP OpenSource report 2025 was published earlier this month. It's a tremendous piece of work from Ulrike, Peter and the rest of the opensource program office team. It covers openness in AI contributions, foundations, communities and culture across the entirety of 2025. It's a great read.

00;00;34;26 - 00;00;42;02

I recommend it to you. The link to the full report is in the description.

00;00;42;04 - 00;01;08;16

¡Hola SAP Developers! As I was enjoying my coffee this morning, I remember a cool new feature in Cloud Integration. I imagine you want to avoid your scripts creating any integration nightmares, especially after an SAP Cloud Integration upgrade, right? You know those custom Groovy scripts that we all love writing, the ones that not necessarily follow best practices. They might use an internal Apache Camel class or unsupported libraries.

00;01;08;18 - 00;01;36;22

Yeah, those that can break spectacularly when there is a major runtime upgrade. The good news is that the Integration Suite team just rolled out a feature called Upgrade Readiness Design Guidelines, and this is included within Cloud Integration, which is activated by default in your Integration tenant. To check your scripts. You need to open the Integration Flow editor, run the guidelines and boom, you'll see which Groovy scripts are non-compliant in the script itself.

00;01;36;25 - 00;02;00;21

There is a problems view that shows you exactly what needs fixing. Think of this feature as a linter for future proofing your scripts. So go ahead and check your scripts to save your future self some headaches. Check out Kamlesh’s blog post to learn more about this feature. ¡Hasta luego!

00;02;00;24 - 00;02;36;25

If you're a real developer, then it means you're constantly learning. And there's good news for CAP developers. As a SAP community member, Martin Stanik has created a new series of great videos on his YouTube channel. The series is called “Become a CAP Developer”. There are many videos already in this series covering core CAP topics such as associations, actions, virtual fields, events, and so on, as well as CAP adjacent to topics such as Fiori Elements, OData draft handling, value helps and more.

00;02;36;27 - 00;02;43;17

Check it out! A link in the description leads to the YouTube playlist. Thanks, Martin.

00;02;43;19 - 00;03;13;15

With the new 0.2.0 release of the UI5 MCP server, a feature gets introduced which allows you to convert your JavaScript UI5 application to TypeScript. Andreas Kuntz is introducing that feature in his blog post on the SAP Community. He's talking about how you can use this feature. He's also sharing best practices and learnings he discovered while using the feature, converting his JavaScript applications to TypeScript.

00;03;13;21 - 00;03;21;07

So if you're interested in this topic, head over to the Community and give his blog post to read.

00;03;21;09 - 00;03;55;17

Yeah, it did win, but it's a less of a problem with us. What are your key takeaways? Yes. Yes. Actually, it was my 1st of October 1st. And, you know, I dedicated myself to it. I took a vacation because, so much content and, I did on almost all the, sessions. It's like, it's like a few pages, you know, you, watch the video, you take a quiz to check your knowledge, and you do exercises.

00;03;55;20 - 00;04;17;20

This is me. Yeah. The most useful part, I think. It's a bit features that you can do all the exercises for, for a trial account. But still, it was great because you took vacation to half the time to join. That is exactly that. That's amazing. Oh, wow. So, yeah. Now you're here in Berlin on ticket on site.

00;04;17;20 - 00;04;43;21

And, what are the highlights from this event? It's also my first ticket, so. Yeah. Wow. It's it's big. A lot of going on. A lot of sessions also session in parallel. So it was sometimes it was, just for people to decide which session to pick. The other one. Yeah. Yeah. A lot of topics about how I and, how you.

00;04;43;24 - 00;05;08;29

Yeah, obviously these topics will be more and more important in the future. But the most important think about this conference, from my opinion, is the people I met so many people, highly professional, open to discuss and share their knowledge. Yeah. I don't make mistakes that people know. You know, their top level, what they're doing.

00;05;09;01 - 00;05;24;10

Yeah, it was very. It's very inspiring. And I am impressed with both, this conference. Yeah. I'm very glad to be feature. Thank you. Yeah. The people. That's what the community's about. Thank you. Thank you. I think a lot of us.

entry.title: Code Connect 2026 is Coming – Mark Your Calendars!
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce the return of Code Connect for its third edition, bringing together three dynamic events - UI5con, reCAP, and HANA Tech Con - under one roof. Mark your calendars for July 13 to 16, 2026, and join us in St. Leon-Rot, Germany, or online.

 

What is Code Connect?

Code Connect creates a unique opportunity to experience three specialized events in one location: UI5con, reCAP, and HANA Tech Con, allowing you to dive deep into different aspects of SAP development. Code Connect is designed for developers at every level: Whether you're an SAP veteran or just starting out, this is your chance to connect, learn, and innovate alongside a vibrant community of developers.

 

Your Week at Code Connect

July 13: Code Jam Sessions and Warmup

Kick things off with our Code Jam sessions - a hands-on way to sharpen your skills before the main event. Afterward, join us for a pre-conference networking event to meet fellow attendees in a relaxed setting.

 

 

 

July 14: UI5con

The official program kicks off with UI5con, bringing together UI5 enthusiasts to share insights, explore the latest innovations, and build new connections. Expect expert sessions, interactive workshops, and plenty of opportunities to engage with the UI5 community.

Learn more about UI5con.

 

 
 
 
 

July 15: reCAP

The next day focuses on the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). At reCAP, developers, customers, and partners meet the CAP Product Team to discuss technical concepts, share project experiences, and explore future possibilities.

Learn more about reCAP.

 

 

 

 

 

July 16: HANA Tech Con

The week concludes with HANA Tech Con. Delve into the HANA universe and join development experts, users, and partners to exchange knowledge and ignite new ideas. If you've ever had questions about HANA that you haven't found answers to, this is your chance to get them resolved.

Learn more about HANA Tech Con.

 

Early Bird Process

Planning a longer trip? We offer a limited number of early bird tickets for attendees who need to arrange travel well in advance.

Check our FAQ document for details on how to secure your ticket.

 

Sponsorship Opportunities

Be a part of Code Connect 2026 and become a sponsor to support the developer community at UI5con, reCAP, and HANA Tech Con. By sponsoring Code Connect, you gain access to a diverse audience spanning front-end developers, backend specialists, and database experts – all in one event.

Read our sponsorship prospectus to check our sponsorship opportunities.

 

Call for Proposals

Our Call for Speakers runs from 26 January to 13 March. As a speaker, you’ll be an active part of Code Connect, shaping the conversation, sharing your expertise, and inspiring the developer community. Don’t wait and submit your session proposal by 13 March at the latest.

  • Call for proposals UI5con
  • Call for proposals reCAP
  • Call for proposals HANA Tech Con

 

Important Dates

  • Call for Proposals: January 26, 2026 to March 13, 2026.
  • Registration Opens: April 10, 2026
  • Agenda Published: Early June 2026
  • Code Connect Week: July 13 to 16, 2026

 

Ready to Connect?

Code Connect 2026 represents more than just learning opportunities. It's about building relationships, sharing knowledge, and being part of a community that's shaping the future of SAP development.

Join us at Code Connect 2026 and be part of a community driving the future of technology. We can't wait to see you there!

entry.title: How We Built a Smart Data Scrambling Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM Using SAP BTP
entry.content:
Introduction

In most organizations, HR data is constantly used in non-production systems—whether for testing, development, training, or troubleshooting. During my work with SAP SuccessFactors landscapes, I’ve often seen how these environments are created quickly by copying data from Production—simply because it’s convenient and readily available.

The problem is that these environments often contain real employee data. This includes names, dates of birth, national IDs, salary details, phone numbers, and home addresses. From my experience, this is where serious privacy risks begin. Even a small oversight can lead to compliance issues under data protection regulations.

At the same time, simply replacing all values with dummy data does not work. Testing requires realistic data. Reporting hierarchies must remain intact. Validations must still pass. Payroll and downstream integrations must continue to work. I’ve personally faced scenarios where masked data looked safe—but broke workflows, reports, or approval processes.

So the real requirement is not just masking—it is smart anonymization that keeps the data functional while removing all sensitive information. This realization is what led to the idea of building a more intelligent, rule-driven, and enterprise-ready data scrambling solution.

Our Approach

To address this challenge, we built a Data Scrambling Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP).

While designing this solution, one of my key goals was to ensure that it feels native to SAP environments—secure, scalable, and easy to integrate. Instead of simply overwriting data with meaningless dummy values, the solution intelligently anonymizes employee-related information using randomized and pattern-based substitutes.

What really mattered to me during development was realism. I wanted test and sandbox systems to behave exactly like production—so validations still pass, workflows still function, and integrations remain intact—while ensuring that no real personal data is exposed.

With this approach, organizations can confidently use non-production systems for testing, training, and demos without compromising data privacy, regulatory compliance, or system integrity.

Key Features and Benefits

1. Comprehensive Data Scrambling

  • The solution scrambles all sensitive HR fields, including employee names, dates of birth, national IDs, phone numbers, email addresses, home addresses, compensation-related information, and more.
  • What makes this approach different is that it preserves referential integrity across key SuccessFactors entities such as Employee Profile, Job Information, Employment Details, and Position data. Relationships between employees, managers, and organizational structures remain intact even after scrambling.
  • As a result, the output remains realistic and fully usable for functional testing, regression testing, and integration testing, ensuring that business processes continue to behave as expected.

2. Configurable Scrambling Rules

  • The solution allows users to define scrambling rules directly from the application at entity level.
  • It supports multiple anonymization techniques, including Randomization, Pattern-based masking, Substitution lists, Pseudonymization
  • These rules are highly flexible and can be tailored to meet specific project requirements, regional regulations, or internal compliance policies.
  • The configuration framework is also designed to support future enhancements, allowing new entities and rule types to be added easily without redesigning the solution.

3. Flexible Execution Options

The solution provides flexible execution modes to suit different project and compliance needs. Users can choose to:

  1. Run full scrambling across the entire dataset
  2. Perform partial scrambling based on Employee groups, Countries, and Selected modules

A simulation mode is also available, allowing users to preview scrambled values before applying them. This helps validate the rules and avoid unintended changes. This selective execution capability ensures that scrambling can be tailored precisely to the required scope without impacting unnecessary data.

4. Realistic and Functional Output

A key design principle of the solution is to ensure that the scrambled data remains fully functional. All transformed values are generated in valid, system-compatible formats, including:

  • Valid date formats
  • Properly structured email addresses and phone numbers
  • Preserved manager–employee hierarchy
  • Country-specific and locale-aware formatting

As a result, business processes, workflows, validations, and integrations continue to function normally—just without exposing any real personal information.

5. Download Scrambled Data

  • The solution provides a secure option to download scrambled data as exportable files for further analysis and audit purposes.
  • Users can easily export anonymized datasets along with summary logs in standard formats such as CSV and Excel.
  • These downloadable reports make it simple to perform data validation, compliance checks, and quality reviews, ensuring transparency and traceability of the scrambling process.

6. Email Notifications

Once a scrambling job is completed, the system automatically sends an email notification to the user. The notification includes:

  • Access to the scrambled output
  • Processing summary
  • Job completion status

This ensures timely communication and eliminates the need for manual follow-ups or repeated system checks. Users can conveniently access the finalized scrambled dataset directly from their email.

7. Secure SAP SuccessFactors Integration

The solution integrates securely with SAP SuccessFactors using:

  • OAuth-secured OData APIs, 
  • SAP Destination Service, and
  • SAP BTP runtime

All data extraction, scrambling, and updates are performed securely within SAP Business Technology Platform.

Architecture and Technical Stack

The Data Scrambling Solution for SAP SuccessFactors is deployed on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and follows a secure, cloud-native architecture.

Users access the application through SAP Build Work Zone, which acts as the central entry point. The App Router loads the user interface from the HTML5 App Repository and handles authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) or a federated identity provider.

Once authenticated, all requests are routed securely to the backend using SAP Destination Service.The backend is built using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). It contains the complete scrambling logic and communicates with SAP SuccessFactors through trusted OData APIs.

To ensure traceability and audit readiness, all activities—such as job execution, errors, and processing details—are logged using SAP Application Logging Service.

This architecture ensures:

  • Secure data flow
  • Proper authorization
  • Controlled access
  • Full auditability

Solution Flow

  1. User launches the Data Scrambling application from SAP Build Work Zone
  2. Frontend UI (SAPUI5) is loaded via the App Router
  3. User authentication happens through SAP IAS
  4. Requests are forwarded to the CAP backend using SAP Destination Service
  5. Backend fetches data from SAP SuccessFactors via OData APIs
  6. Scrambling rules are applied to sensitive fields
  7. Logs and audit details are recorded
  8. Final scrambled output is generated and shared

Business Value

  • This solution delivers strong business value by addressing both compliance and operational challenges.
  • It protects employee privacy by ensuring that real personal data is never exposed in test, development, or training environments. This significantly reduces the risk of data leaks and regulatory violations.
  • At the same time, it keeps test environments realistic and fully functional. By preserving data formats, relationships, and system behavior, teams can continue to test workflows, reports, and integrations without disruption.
  • The solution also reduces manual effort by automating the entire anonymization process. There is no longer a need for custom scripts, ad-hoc masking, or manual cleanup activities.
  • By eliminating manual handling of sensitive data, it lowers the risk of human errors that often occur during data copying or cleansing.
  • Finally, the solution is designed to be secure and scalable, making it suitable for both small teams and large enterprise landscapes.

Conclusion

Using real HR data in non-production systems introduces serious privacy and compliance risks. At the same time, relying on unrealistic dummy data makes testing ineffective and unreliable.

This Data Scrambling Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM bridges that gap by providing a secure, realistic, and automated way to anonymize sensitive employee information. By preserving data structures, formats, and relationships, the solution enables teams to confidently test, train, and troubleshoot without exposing real personal data.

From my own experience working with SuccessFactors integrations and test environments, I’ve seen how much time and risk this approach can save. Instead of manually cleaning data or writing one-off scripts, teams can now rely on a standardized, scalable, and auditable solution.

Ultimately, this is not just about compliance—it’s about enabling innovation safely. When teams no longer have to worry about data privacy risks, they can focus on building, improving, and delivering better solutions with confidence.

entry.title: Setting Up PostgreSQL for SAP BTP Development: A Practical Guide
entry.content:
Setting Up PostgreSQL for SAP BTP Development: A Practical Guide

Part 1 of a series — next up: implementing OAuth with XSUAA and PostgreSQL

Not every BTP application needs HANA Cloud. PostgreSQL on SAP BTP is a managed database service running on hyperscaler infrastructure. Credentials arrive via VCAP_SERVICES in Cloud Foundry or Kubernetes secrets in Kyma.

When Should You Choose PostgreSQL Over HANA Cloud?

Need analytics or calculation views? → HANA Cloud
Need MTX support? → HANA Cloud


Everything else? → PostgreSQL

That's really it. If you're not using HANA-specific features, you're paying premium prices for a standard relational database. PostgreSQL handles CRUD just as well at a fraction of the cost.

Provisioning PostgreSQL on SAP BTP

Prerequisites: Setting Up Entitlements

Before you can create a PostgreSQL instance, your subaccount needs the right entitlements. For trial account it's set up by default.

Method 1: Creating via BTP Cockpit (UI)

This approach works well when you're getting started or creating a one-off instance.

Step 1: Navigate to your Cloud Foundry Space

  • BTP Cockpit → Subaccount → Cloud Foundry → Spaces → [Your Space]

Step 2: Open the Service Marketplace

  • Click Services in the left menu → Service Marketplace

Step 3: Find PostgreSQL

  • Search for "PostgreSQL" or look for "PostgreSQL, hyperscaler option"
  • Click on the tile

Step 4: Create a new instance

  • Click the Create button

Step 5: Name your instance psql-dev and click Create

The provisioning takes 5-15 minutes.

Method 2: Creating via CF CLI

cf login -a https://api.cf.eu10.hana.ondemand.com
cf target -o my-organization -s my-space

# Create the service instance
cf create-service postgresql-db free psql-dev

# Monitor the creation progress
cf service psql-dev

The cf service command will show status like "create in progress" initially, then "create succeeded" when ready.

Getting Your Database Credentials

Once your instance is created, you need credentials to connect. There are two approaches depending on your use case.

For Applications: Service Binding

If you're deploying an application to Cloud Foundry, the standard approach is binding:

# Bind the service to your app
cf bind-service my-application psql-dev

# Restage to pick up the new binding
cf restage my-application

After binding, your application receives credentials via the VCAP_SERVICES environment variable. Here's what that looks like (simplified):

{
  "postgresql-db": [{
    "credentials": {
      "hostname": "postgres-abc123.database.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
      "port": "5432",
      "dbname": "postgres",
      "username": "user_xyz",
      "password": "generated-secure-password",
      "uri": "postgres://user_xyz:password@hostname:5432/postgres?sslmode=verify-full",
      "sslcert": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n...",
      "sslrootcert": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n..."
    }
  }]
}

Important: Notice the SSL certificates. BTP PostgreSQL requires SSL connections — this isn't optional.

For Local Development

BTP PostgreSQL isn't publicly accessible from your laptop. For local development, just run PostgreSQL locally via Docker — it's faster anyway.

Option 1: Local PostgreSQL via Docker

For CAP apps, use the pg.yaml shown in the CAP section below with cds watch --profile pg. For non-CAP apps, the same Docker setup works — just connect to localhost:5432 with user/password postgres.

Option 2: Deploy pgAdmin on BTP

Need to browse your BTP database with a GUI? Deploy pgAdmin directly on Cloud Foundry — it runs inside the network and connects without tunneling.

manifest.yml:

applications:
- name: pgadmin-web
  memory: 1G
  disk_quota: 1G
  health-check-type: process
  docker:
    image: dpage/pgadmin4:latest
  random-route: true
  env:
    PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL: admin@admin.com
    PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: admin123
cf push

Open the route URL from the output, login with the credentials above, and add your server using the hostname/port/username/password from your service key.

Connecting from Different Application Types

Now for the practical part — actually connecting your code to the database. I'll cover the major scenarios.

Scenario 1: CAP Application (Node.js)

The @cap-js/postgres package provides official PostgreSQL support.

Setup:

cds add postgres

Local development with Docker:

# pg.yaml
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:alpine
    environment: { POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres }
    ports: ['5432:5432']
docker compose -f pg.yaml up -d
cds watch --profile pg

The @cap-js/postgres package comes with default credentials under profile [pg] that match the Docker defaults — no configuration needed.

Scenario 2: Plain Node.js Application (Express, Fastify, etc.)

Here's where things get interesting for non-CAP developers. You can use PostgreSQL with any Node.js framework using the standard pg package.

Installation:

npm install pg @sap/xsenv

Connection setup:

// db.js - Database connection module
const { Pool } = require('pg');
const xsenv = require('@sap/xsenv');

// Load environment (works both locally with default-env.json and on CF)
xsenv.loadEnv();

// Get PostgreSQL credentials from VCAP_SERVICES
const pgCredentials = xsenv.getServices({ 
  postgres: { label: 'postgresql-db' } 
}).postgres;

// Create connection pool with SSL configuration
const pool = new Pool({
  host: pgCredentials.hostname,
  port: parseInt(pgCredentials.port, 10),
  database: pgCredentials.dbname,
  user: pgCredentials.username,
  password: pgCredentials.password,
});

// Test the connection on startup
pool.query('SELECT NOW()')
  .then(() => console.log('✅ PostgreSQL connected'))
  .catch(err => console.error('❌ PostgreSQL connection failed:', err));

module.exports = pool;

Using it in your routes:

// routes/users.js
const express = require('express');
const pool = require('../db');
const router = express.Router();

router.get('/users', async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const result = await pool.query('SELECT id, name, email FROM users');
    res.json(result.rows);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Query error:', err);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Database error' });
  }
});

router.post('/users', async (req, res) => {
  const { name, email } = req.body;
  try {
    const result = await pool.query(
      'INSERT INTO users (name, email) VALUES ($1, $2) RETURNING *',
      [name, email]
    );
    res.status(201).json(result.rows[0]);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Insert error:', err);
    res.status(500).json({ error: 'Database error' });
  }
});

module.exports = router;

What's Next: Building OAuth Authentication with XSUAA

Now that you have PostgreSQL running on BTP, we're ready to build something practical.

In the next article, we'll implement OAuth 2.0 authentication using XSUAA and PostgreSQL — without relying on the CAP framework.

See you in Part 2!

Resources

  • CAP: Using PostgreSQL
  • PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - Hyperscaler Option
  • @cap-js/postgres package

entry.title: SAP Developer News, January 22nd, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

UI5Con Bangalore 

  • UI5con Bangalore: https://openui5.org/ui5con/bengaluru2026/index.html 

 SAP BTP Platform Automation Roundtable #1 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/customer-amp-partner-roundtable-for-sap-btp-platform-automation-february/ba-p/14306394  
  • Registration https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/ccc79b7d-c96b-4564-a1a3-361b82789171@42f7676c-f455-423c-82f6-dc2d99791af7 

Event Mesh capability monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM 

  • Monitoring Event Mesh from SAP Integration Suite with SAP Cloud ALM: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/monitoring-event-mesh-from-sap-integration-suite-with-sap-cloud-alm/ba-p/14309869  

SAPUI5 Roadmap Explorer 

  • Link to the Roadmap: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800001361&range=CURRENT-LAST&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q1%202026 

Code Connect 2026 

  • Code Connect website https://code-connect.dev/  

Devtoberfest Winners Interview - Gert Mertens 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 UI5con Bangalore 

0:45 SAP BTP Platform Automation Roundtable #1 

1:28 Event Mesh capability monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM 

2:19 SAPUI5 Roadmap Explorer 

3:23 Code Connect 2026 

4:48 Devtoberfest Winners Interview - Gert Mertens 

Transcript

00;00;00;25 - 00;00;06;20

This is the SAP Developer News for January 22nd, 2026.

00;00;06;23 - 00;00;09;18

We have kick started the first CodeJam of 2026 in

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;25;10

Hyderabad. We have two more CodeJams on SAP Gen AI and ABAP Cloud. Do let us know in which city you want to have the next CodeJam. And I'll just have one more news to share. UI5Con is coming to Bangalore after many years. So all UI5 enthusiast

00;00;25;10 - 00;00;44;18

Just watch out this space for UI5Con Bangalore happening at SAP Labs Innovation Park, Bangalore on February 28th. Mark the date it's February 28th. All the information about the registration can be in the link provided in the description. Thank you.

00;00;44;21 - 00;01;15;26

The first monthly customer and partner roundtable for SAP BTP Platform Automation is set to take place next month. Tuesday, the 10th of February at 1100 hours CET. BTP Platform automation. That's right. Topics such as the BTP CLI and Terraform. Two of my favorite topics during the session. There'll be Q&A. There'll be a round of updates, some demos and also a look ahead for the rest of 2026.

00;01;15;28 - 00;01;28;20

Everyone's welcome. You just need to register. It's free. Christian's blog post has all the details, so head over there and check it out. Link in the description.

00;01;28;22 - 00;01;58;22

Hola developers! Are you using the event mesh capability and you also use cloud them for monitoring? Well, here's some news for you. It is now possible to monitor the event mesh capability directly in SAP cloud ALM, meaning that you can now not just monitor your cloud integration tenant, but also your event mesh capability program for the broker. You can monitor the broker health active connections, message consumers and message queues.

00;01;58;25 - 00;02;18;20

The setup is pretty straightforward. If your integration suite is already in cloud ALM, then you just hit the scope selector in the health monitoring app, toggle on the event Mesh capability and that's it. Want to learn more about it? Check out Shivani’s blog post on SAP community. Hasta luego.

00;02;18;22 - 00;02;44;23

Developers love road maps, particularly when we're not the ones that have to deliver on them. They're kind of like wish lists that are going to be fulfilled. So it's fun to check out what's, on upcoming road maps. This month we see the updated SAPUI5 road map and contains lots of new functionality around Fiori web components and other capabilities that SAPUI5 makes possible.

00;02;44;26 - 00;03;11;17

Just to, give you an example of a few of them. For instance, there's a new geo map control plan in the road map, that will be, metadata driven control. And there is a planned MDC server for, UI5 web components. And that's just one of many things planned in Q1, Q1 being the section that has the most details.

00;03;11;20 - 00;03;23;26

But if you're a UI five developer, I'm sure this is a roadmap you're going to want to check out to see what things you can take advantage of in the near future.

00;03;23;28 - 00;04;05;21

It's only January, but we can already happily announce that CodeConnect is happening again this year. Undeniably a key in-person event for developers in the SAP ecosphere. This year, it includes three classic one day conferences. UI5Con on Tuesday, reCAP on Wednesday and HANA TechCon on Thursday, and starting the CodeConnect week off on Monday. I'm not one, not two, but three CodeJams one on CAP topics, one on Gen AI coding on the SAP rapid one model and one on UI5.

00;04;05;23 - 00;04;35;03

When is CodeConnect, I hear you ask? Well, it's pretty much right in the center of July. It's most of the week beginning the 13th of July. It's been in St. Leon/ROT in Germany as usual at the SAP campus. There we can mingle and chat with like minded developers, listen to and maybe even give sessions and just generally geek out. It's become a must attend event for folks like you and like me.

00;04;35;05 - 00;04;49;01

Anyway, head over to the CodeConnect website to learn more and if you are able to become a sponsor, then get in touch with the organizers there. Lovely people and thank you.

00;04;49;03 - 00;05;13;20

It's so I'm happy to right back from at TechEd. So what else do you join? And I'm one of the ministers. What are you? Key takeaways. The main things. What did you like? What did you like? What did I learn from the photographers I really liked? Especially this year that, you had, like, all the five tracks, so that you could really choose what you wanted to, to focus on.

00;05;13;22 - 00;05;37;05

And also, the photography is also really focused on both juniors and more experienced people. This read something for everybody and also with the funny sense it's it's really yeah, you learn a lot, but also doing it via for example, the puzzle you learn a little of things there. So it was really fun to do and yeah, to be able to just have like a gamification around it.

00;05;37;08 - 00;05;57;24

Yeah. That really speaks to me as a developer. So yeah, that it was me. A lot of fun and a lot of learning to do. Yeah. And now you're here and then. Oh yeah. So many highlights. What did you like most? Especially at the community, after, Covid, of course. Yeah. Being back in a community meeting, the mentors.

00;05;57;24 - 00;06;17;19

Meeting the advocates. Yeah. Especially that. And also with AI, the big buzzwords, seeing what is a piece doing with it? Yeah, all of those agents that they're building, it's really, really, really nice to just be here and feel the vibe and seeing what is in the future for for it. Yeah, that's what I really like about it I guess right now.

00;06;17;20 - 00;06;20;10

Yeah. But thank you guys. No problem.
